_comment: Trigger eval for devil-advocating skill. 10 should-trigger + 10 should-not-trigger = 20 cases. Negative cases test the line between 'red-team my position' (fires) and 'give me a balanced view' or 'argue this for a debate class' (does not fire). The skill produces one-sided opposition; balanced critique is a different ask.

evals.query: /devil-advocating
I think we should adopt microservices for our 10-person team. red team this for me — what's the strongest case against?
I'm convinced we should move to a 4-day work week. argue against me as hard as you can.
stress test my plan to leave my job and start a consulting firm. don't be balanced, be the harshest critic
what's the strongest case against this thesis: that AI coding assistants will commoditize junior developer work in 3 years?
I'm leaning toward selling the house and renting. tell me why I'm wrong.
argue against my position that we should kill the legacy product line
I want a smart opponent's view on why I shouldn't take this acquisition offer. give me their best case.
tear apart my pitch deck thesis. I need to hear what investors are going to push back with before Friday.
I genuinely believe remote-first is better for our company. convince me I'm wrong.
what are the pros and cons of microservices for small teams?
give me both sides of the work-from-home debate
I'm preparing for a debate class — argue the AGAINST side of 'social media has been net-positive for society'
what's an argument someone might make against electric vehicles?
explain why some economists think rent control is bad policy
I had a fight with my partner. they think I'm wrong about something — help me see their side
play devil's advocate but be balanced about it
I don't have a position yet — I'm trying to figure out what I think about this. show me both sides
write me a counter-argument to this essay I'm grading
what counter-arguments do startup investors typically use against bootstrapped founders?
